creator: nemysis@gmx.ch
text:
On Fri, 14 Sep 2012 08:24:32 -0400
"Jason E. Hale" <jhale@freebsd.org> wrote:

> On Friday, September 14, 2012 13:52:46 nemysis wrote:
> > On Fri, 14 Sep 2012 04:18:22 -0400
> > 
> > "Jason E. Hale" <jhale@freebsd.org> wrote:
> > > > +# Pixmaps
> > > > +   ${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/icons/${PORTNAME}.png
> > > > ${PREFIX}/share/pixmaps/ +
> > > 
> > > Is it really neccessary to install an additional icon in share/pixmaps?
> > > The game and .desktop file seem to work fine without it.  If there is a
> > > good reason, I would recommend creating a symlink to
> > > share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/connectagram.png, e.g. ${LN} -sf
> > > ${PREFIX}/share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/connectagram.png
> > > ${PREFIX}/share/pixmaps/connectagram.png
> > Yes this works good, not all users use desktop files.
> > 
> > Some use Window Manager (here FluxBox) and need pixmaps in
> > ${PREFIX}/share/pixmaps/
> 
> Ok, we'll keep that.

Thanks
> > > Also, the pkg-plist should include the data files installed by the port. 
> > > The PORTDATA macro will break the port if the user has NOPORTDATA
> > > defined.
> > I have removed PORTDATA macro, why is only one file "words" there.
> > 
> > Have changed pkg-plist which now include all files.
> 
> Looks fine, but share/connectagram should be using in place of %%DATADIR%% 
> because if the user redefines DATADIR the port is still going to install in 
> share/connectagram.

Look this please

grep -r %%DATADIR%% /usr/ports

I use only %%DATADIR%% for /usr/local/share/package

When user wish to change DATADIR, better is to install manually.
 
> I'm fine with everything else.  I also took ports/171364 for games/tanglet 
> which has pretty much the same issues as this port.  Are you OK with me 
> applying these changes to that port as well?

Hve just sendet a diff for Tangle now.

But only with 
 
> - Jason
